title: Unit 06 Pretest
description: Diagnostic pretest for Financial Planning, Insurance, and Life Decisions.

questions.prompt: Why is financial planning for life stages important?

choices.text: Because future expenses and goals can change over time
choices.feedback: Correct. Financial planning helps people prepare for changing needs across life stages.

choices.text: Because all financial needs stay the same throughout life
choices.feedback: Financial needs often change as education, work, and living situations change.

choices.text: Because planning removes the need to make later decisions
choices.feedback: Planning supports later decisions, but it does not remove the need to make them.

choices.text: Because planning guarantees that no financial problems will happen
choices.feedback: Planning can reduce risk, but it does not guarantee perfect outcomes.

questions.prompt: Insurance can help reduce the financial impact of unexpected events.

choices.text: False
choices.feedback: Insurance is meant to reduce the financial impact of covered risks and losses.

choices.text: True
choices.feedback: Correct. Insurance helps people manage financial risk when unexpected events occur.

questions.prompt: Which example is a major financial decision?

choices.text: Choosing what snack to buy once
choices.feedback: A one-time snack purchase is not usually considered a major financial decision.

choices.text: Deciding whether to rent lower-cost housing or choose a more expensive option
choices.feedback: This is a major financial decision, but the correct answer in this set is placed elsewhere.

choices.text: Deciding whether to rent lower-cost housing or choose a more expensive option
choices.feedback: Correct. Housing decisions can have large and lasting effects on a financial plan.

choices.text: Borrowing a pencil in class
choices.feedback: That is not a major financial decision.

questions.prompt: What is one sign of financial responsibility?

choices.text: Ignoring bills until they become urgent
choices.feedback: Ignoring obligations increases the risk of fees and other consequences.

choices.text: Making decisions without thinking about consequences
choices.feedback: Responsible choices require attention to consequences.

choices.text: Assuming someone else will solve every money problem
choices.feedback: Financial responsibility includes personal accountability.

choices.text: Following through on obligations and considering consequences
choices.feedback: Correct. Responsibility includes meeting obligations and thinking ahead.

questions.prompt: A transportation choice can affect other parts of a financial plan.

choices.text: True
choices.feedback: Correct. Transportation costs can affect savings, housing options, and other priorities.

choices.text: False
choices.feedback: Transportation is a major expense that can affect the rest of a financial plan.

questions.prompt: Which type of insurance is most closely connected to medical expenses?

choices.text: Auto insurance
choices.feedback: Auto insurance is associated with vehicle-related risk.

choices.text: Health insurance
choices.feedback: Correct. Health insurance is connected to medical costs and care.

choices.text: Property insurance
choices.feedback: Property insurance is associated with homes or belongings, not medical expenses.

choices.text: Travel entertainment insurance
choices.feedback: That is not the best match for basic medical expenses in this lesson.

questions.prompt: If a person saves $90 each month for 4 months, how much will be saved?

choices.feedback: Multiply the monthly amount by the number of months carefully.
This total is too low.
Correct. $90 times 4 equals $360.
This total is too high.

questions.prompt: Which choice best shows accountability in personal finance?

choices.text: Pretending that consequences do not matter
choices.feedback: Accountability requires recognizing the consequences of choices.

choices.text: Blaming every financial outcome on other people
choices.feedback: Accountability involves taking ownership of decisions.

choices.text: Making money decisions only for immediate comfort
choices.feedback: Responsible decisions consider longer-term impact.

choices.text: Accepting the results of choices and learning from them
choices.feedback: Correct. Accountability means owning the results of financial choices and improving future decisions.
